video_title: Guy Fieri Circles Back for BIG Flavor Texas Barbecue 🔥 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: The Smoke Shack
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in San Antonio, Texas, and yes, ladies and gentlemen, it is always bigger in Texas. And that couldn't be truer for this joint I found in 2018, with a few different spots and scratch-made everything. But it's the big flavors that are still driving in the crowds. The Smoke Shack, barbecue, and southern kitchen. And perfected by Chris Conger, who's conquered the art of the smoker with his legit spin on Texas barbecue.

dishes.description: Brisket rubbed with salt, pepper, chili powder, garlic pepper, paprika, onion powder, brown sugar, smoked 12-14 hours. Served with barbecue sauce (ketchup, apple cider vinegar, molasses, steak sauce) and mustard sauce (barbecue sauce, Worcestershire, garlic pepper, barbecue rub, black pepper, honey, cinnamon). Beans made with pinto beans, onion, jalapenos, garlic, salt, pepper, crushed tomatoes, chili powder. Layered with cheese, beans, brisket, barbecue sauce, and corn chips.
dishes.guy_response: The beans have got great flavor, great texture. The corn chips just add a nice little crunch, but it's the barbecue that really sings. Delicious. Outstanding. Best in town.

dishes.description: Chicken wings dry-rubbed with citrus, smoked for about an hour and a half at 250 degrees, then deep-fried to order. Served with a cranberry pepper sauce made with cranberries, apple cider vinegar, orange juice, lime juice, sugar, salt, allspice, rehydrated chili piquin, and pectin for thickness.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Good cranberry. Little bit of heat. Actually tastes like I'm having smoked turkey, cranberry wing sauce. Right? You are on fire. It's the perfect texture on a perfect wing. Cranberry sauce, a little kick in it, really awesome. I'm a bit of a wing connoisseur. And probably the best I've ever had.

dishes.description: A take on a Reuben featuring chopped pastrami (brisket brined for seven days with salt, brown sugar, garlic flakes, coriander, allspice, black pepper, sodium nitrate, then rubbed and smoked overnight, rough chopped). Served with a slaw (instead of sauerkraut) made with roasted jalapenos and Thousand Island slaw (ketchup, mayo, relish, red onion, hot sauce, salt, pepper, green and purple cabbage, carrots).
dishes.guy_response: Chopped pastrami, texturally outrageous. Right. And you really get some great pastrami out of this. I mean, you brined this the right way. You've got enough. Coriander and black pepper in this, yeah. Dynamite, bro. That chopped meat and just an extra kind of spice to it, it's great. You get a little bit of that slaw mixed with the Thousand Island dressing, it's incredible.
